reviews.review_msg: Loved the lectures and the topics/class discussion. Very interesting course.

Stuart is a very fun guy. His classes have a great vibe and you can tell he loves talking to his students. He sparks meaningful discussions in live classes, however my only gripe is that his prerecorded asynchronous lectures are very dull. They are boring and it often feels like he talks about literally nothing for 1 hour.

Dr. Poyntz (Stuart) has by far been one of the best professors I have had the joy to take a class with. His style is highly engaging; both the lectures and readings are easy to follow and can easily be compared to real-life scenarios. A majority of this class is showcasing learning through discussion posts. I thoroughly enjoyed CMNS 221.

Stuart is a truly excellent professor and academic. He is very eloquent and always prepared, making his lectures very interesting and engaging. He is rooting for his students and wants them to do well - even beyond the classroom, Stuart strives for success for his students who reach out.

By far my favourite course ever taken at SFU. Stuart is sooooo inspiring and intelligent. He seems to talk a lot, however he ends class earlier which is awesome. The material covered in lecture was super relevant and relatable. Final assignment was super cool - not your typical paper (but you can if you want to). He wants students to do well!!!!!!!

He uses outdated services (Canvas) to register for course discussions allowing other students to erase names. This happened to me & my friend more than 3 times and Stuart did nothing about it claiming this is the 'first time in 7 years' this has happened. Instead of addressing the issue he blamed Canvas and continued to let these problems occur.

This happened to me & my friend more than 3 times and Stuart did nothing about it claiming this is the 'first time in 7 years' this has happened. Instead of addressing the issue he blamed Canvas and continued to let these problems continue

Stuart is awesome! The content for this course can be dry at times, but he does a great job at making it interesting by showing lots of movie clips and relating it to real world examples. Wants a lot of class participation but isn't awkward about it like some profs can be. Learned about half the class's names by the end of the semester. Love him!

Poyntz is probably the most prepared lecturer I've ever witnessed. He is passionate about what he teaches and he instantly captivates people. He even went to great lengths to bring in Shad, a popular hip-hop artist to come do a guest lecture. I can't wait to take more courses with him!

Believe the hype — this guy is one of the most engaging and well-prepared lecturers I've ever had. It's obvious he's genuinely passionate about the subject matter, and he manages to make even the driest topics interesting without going off on crazy tangents like other profs often do. If you get the chance, take a class with this guy.
